prompts: In a simple mechanical pulley system with a single wheel, a rope runs over the pulley with two masses on either end. The forces involved are gravitational forces on each mass, the tension in the rope, and the force exerted by the pulley on the system, which typically counters gravitational pull. Tension is uniform throughout the rope if massless and frictionless. The gravitational force on each mass is the product of mass and gravitational acceleration (mg). If the masses are unequal, the system will accelerate towards the heavier mass, with net force determined by subtracting the forces on the lighter mass from those on the heavier mass.
